title: refactor: align encoder configuration with Chromium WebCodecs behavior
body:
Based on Chromium source code analysis, update encoder settings to match
Chromium's WebCodecs implementation philosophy:

Encoder changes:
- libx264: Use preset=superfast + tune=zerolatency (constant, not latencyMode-dependent)
- libx265: Use preset=ultrafast (avoids lookahead/bframe conflicts)
- libvpx-vp8: Use cpu-used=-6 constant (matches Chromium)
- libvpx-vp9: Use cpu-used=7 constant + row-mt=1 (matches Chromium)
- libaom-av1: Use cpu-used=9/7 based on latencyMode + row-mt=1 (matches Chromium)

Rate control fixes:
- Always set rc_buffer_size for x264/x265 (required for VBV bitrate enforcement)
- Default buffer size to 1x bitrate for CBR, 2x for VBR

Other changes:
- Add benchmark scripts to compare with webcodecs-polyfill
- Fix lint issues: remove await from muxer.flush() (returns void)
- Fix type assertion for Promise in video-encoder-behavior test

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

<!-- CURSOR_SUMMARY -->
---

> [!NOTE]
> Brings encoder behavior in line with Chromium WebCodecs and tightens rate control, with new real-world benchmarks and tooling updates.
> 
> - Encoder: Add `apply_sw_encoder_options` and use per-encoder defaults (`libx264` preset=superfast+tune=zerolatency, `libx265` preset=ultrafast, `libvpx-vp8` cpu-used=-6, `libvpx-vp9` cpu-used=7+`row-mt`=1, `libaom-av1` cpu-used 9/7 by `latencyMode`); apply options for both configure/reconfigure and SW fallback
> - Latency/GOP: Use `latencyMode` to choose GOP/B-frames (realtime: `gop=10`, `bframes=0`; quality: `gop=60`, `bframes=2`)
> - Rate control: Always set `rc_buffer_size`; default to `bitrate` for CBR and `2×bitrate` for VBR
> - Tests: Replace `await muxer.flush()` with sync `flush()`, fix a Promise type assertion in WPT test
> - Benchmarks/tooling: Remove old `benchmark/bench.ts`; add `benchmark/bench-napi.ts`, `bench-polyfill.ts`, and `compare.ts` (real video decode/encode, polyfill comparison); add `build.ts` using `oxfmt`; update npm scripts/deps and lockfile
> - Misc: Ensure Windows `rustflags` for i686, minor formatting in `Cargo.toml`

title: Refactor environment variable management with centralized validation
body:
## Summary

This PR refactors environment variable management to use a centralized, type-safe approach with startup validation. The changes improve code consistency, eliminate redundant validation checks, and fix several ESLint warnings.

### Key Changes

**Centralized Environment Management:**
- Created `lib/env.ts` with Map-based environment variable structure
- All env vars now accessed via `env.get('VAR_NAME')` instead of `process.env.VAR_NAME`
- Added helper functions: `isDevelopment()`, `isProduction()`, `getLlamaCloudApiKey()`
- Sentinel value `'mandatory-env-not-set'` for required variables

**Startup Validation:**
- Added `instrumentation.ts` to validate environment once at server startup
- Removed 9+ redundant `validateEnv()` calls from services and API routes
- Server fails fast at startup if required variables are missing

**Code Quality Improvements:**
- Fixed ESLint warnings in React components (ProjectDocuments, ProjectIndexSelector)
- Added proper useCallback and useEffect dependency arrays
- Refactored LlamaIndexService import paths
- Simplified `next.config.ts` by removing explicit env var exposure

### Files Changed (24 files)

**Core Environment Management:**
- `lib/env.ts` - Centralized environment configuration (major refactor)
- `instrumentation.ts` - One-time startup validation
- `next.config.ts` - Simplified configuration

**Services Updated:**
- OpenAI services: `openai-question-extractor.ts`, `multi-step-response-service.ts`
- LlamaCloud services: `llamacloud-client.ts`, `llamacloud-connection-service.ts`, `llamacloud-documents-service.ts`
- LlamaIndex/Parse: `llamaindex-service.ts` (renamed from llama-index-service.ts), `llamaparse-service.ts`
- Supabase utilities: `client.ts`, `middleware.ts`, `server.ts` (both in lib/utils and utils/)
- Database: `lib/db.ts`

**API Routes & Components:**
- API routes: `generate-response-multistep`, `health`, `llamacloud/projects`, `organizations`, `projects/[projectId]/indexes`
- Login: `app/login/actions.ts`
- Components: `ProjectDocuments.tsx`, `ProjectIndexSelector.tsx`

### Benefits

- **Type Safety:** All env access goes through a single typed interface
- **Performance:** Environment validated once at startup instead of per-request
- **Maintainability:** Consistent pattern across entire codebase
- **Developer Experience:** Clear error messages for missing environment variables
- **Code Quality:** ESLint checks pass with no warnings

### Testing

- ✅ TypeScript compilation passes
- ✅ ESLint checks pass with no warnings
- ✅ All services correctly use `env.get()` pattern
- ✅ Startup validation works as expected

   - ハードコード値をConfig.Author定数に置き換え
   - author-name, author-job, author-github, author-twitter, author-stackoverflow

3. jsonLdPersonFieldの多言語対応（Contexts.Field）
   - constFieldからfieldに変更

   - デフォルト: 日本語

4. jsonLdArticleFieldの改善

- DRY原則に準拠（ハードコーディング重複の解消）

title: Fix checkout flow and refactor Stripe module
body:
## Summary
Fixes the "Checkout not available" error that was preventing users from upgrading to Pro/Max plans, and refactors the Stripe module into a modular directory structure for better separation of server-only and client-safe code.

## Changes

### Stripe Module Refactoring
- **Modular Structure** - Split `lib/stripe.ts` into `lib/stripe/` directory
  - `lib/stripe/index.ts` - Server-only Stripe client and functions
  - `lib/stripe/plans.ts` - Client-safe plan constants and types
- **Server/Client Separation** - Prevents server env vars from leaking to client bundles
- **New Helper** - Added `getPriceIdForPlan()` function for server-side price ID resolution

### Checkout Flow Fix
- **Root Cause** - Client-side code checked `priceId` from env vars, but non-NEXT_PUBLIC vars are undefined on client
- **Solution** - Removed client-side priceId validation, use server-side resolution instead
- **User Creation** - Added just-in-time user creation for Clerk users without database records
- **Error Handling** - Improved error messages for Stripe configuration issues

### Upgrade CTA Improvements
- **In-Table Upgrade Row** - New `UpgradeRow` component for clean, non-aggressive upsell
- **Contextual Messaging** - Shows different messages based on limit status
- **Tier-Aware** - Only visible for FREE tier users

### Testing & Documentation
- Added `upgrade-row.test.tsx` for component testing
- Added `checkout-flow.test.ts` for integration testing
- Implementation plan documenting the fix approach
- Research notes on "User not found" error investigation

## Test Plan
- [x] Unit tests pass
- [x] TypeScript compiles without errors
- [x] Checkout API uses server-side priceId resolution
- [x] UpgradeRow component renders correctly
- [ ] Manual Stripe checkout flow verification (sandbox)

## Files Modified
- `lib/stripe/index.ts` - New server-only module
- `lib/stripe/plans.ts` - New client-safe constants
- `app/api/checkout/direct/route.ts` - Use getPriceIdForPlan()
- `app/api/user/subscription/route.ts` - Just-in-time user creation
- `hooks/use-subscription.ts` - Simplified checkout flow
- `components/dashboard/tickers-table/upgrade-row.tsx` - New component
- `components/dashboard/tickers-table/index.ts` - Export upgrade row

---

title: refactor(stack): use as_file() for package resource copying
body:
Change git hook installation to use importlib.resources.as_file()
context manager for copying resources. This ensures compatibility
with zip/PEX-style package installations where resources aren't
directly accessible as filesystem paths.

- Rename _get_script_resource_path() to _get_script_resource()
  returning Traversable instead of str
- Add _get_wrapper_resource() helper
- Update _script_needs_update() to accept Traversable
- Use as_file() context manager in _install_git_hook() and
  ensure_hooks_updated()

This makes git hook handling consistent with Claude hook handling
which already uses as_file().

title: Post-Phase-6: BigInt base64, unpadded base64, Date rework, nibble optimization
body:
## Summary

Post-Phase-6 data model changes: BigInt base64 wire format, unpadded base64 convention, Date rework with new temporal types, and BigInt encoding performance optimization.

### BigInt base64 wire format

Switch the `BigInt@1` JSON wire format from decimal string to unpadded base64 of two's-complement big-endian bytes.

**Spec changes** (`1-storable-values.md`):
- `BigInt@1` state changed from decimal string to base64 of minimal two's-complement big-endian bytes
- Added base64 encoding convention: standard alphabet, no `=` padding
- Updated `Bytes@1` and `canonicalHash` doc comment to reference the convention
- Examples: `0n` -> `"AA"`, `1n` -> `"AQ"`, `-1n` -> `"/w"`, `128n` -> `"AIA"`

**Code changes** (`packages/memory/`):
- New shared module `bigint-encoding.ts`: BigInt <-> two's-complement big-endian bytes, with base64 helpers
- Updated `type-handlers.ts`: `BigInt@1` serialize/deserialize uses new encoding
- Refactored `canonical-hash.ts`: imports shared BigInt encoding instead of inline implementation
- 58 new test steps in `bigint-encoding-test.ts` (encode/decode/round-trip/base64)
- Updated `serialization-test.ts` with new wire format expectations

### Date rework: StorableEpochNsec + StorableEpochDays

Replace `StorableDate` (wraps JS `Date`) with two new temporal types wrapping bigint values:
- **`StorableEpochNsec`**: signed nanoseconds from the POSIX Epoch
- **`StorableEpochDays`**: signed days from the POSIX Epoch

**Spec changes** (`1-storable-values.md`, `2-canonical-hash-byte-format.md`):
- Replaced `StorableDate` / `Date@1` sections with `StorableEpochNsec` / `EpochNsec@1` and `StorableEpochDays` / `EpochDays@1`
- Updated canonical hash spec to remove `TAG_DATE` ISO-string encoding; new types hash via generic `TAG_INSTANCE` + `[DECONSTRUCT]` -> bigint path

**Code changes** (`packages/memory/`):
- Removed `StorableDate`, added `StorableEpochNsec` and `StorableEpochDays` in `storable-native-instances.ts`
- New type tags `EpochNsec@1` and `EpochDays@1` in `type-tags.ts`
- `Date` -> `StorableEpochNsec` conversion: `BigInt(date.getTime()) * 1_000_000n`
- Updated class registry, serialization, and canonical hash tests

### Performance optimization

Replaced `parseInt` with manual nibble-decoding in BigInt hex-to-bytes conversion for ~1.2-2.8x speedup depending on BigInt magnitude.

### Test results

- 107 tests, 641 steps, 0 failures

title: [prover] Fix behavioral predicates on opaque functions
body:
## Description

Fix Boogie compilation error ("call to undeclared procedure") when behavioral predicates (`result_of<f>`, `aborts_of<f>`) are used on opaque functions.

The `$apply` procedure (which dispatches closure calls) emitted a direct `call` to the target function's Boogie procedure. But opaque functions have no Boogie procedure body generated, causing the compilation error. The fix detects opaque targets in the closure variant loop and uses behavioral predicates (`$aborts_of` evaluator + `$result_of` function) instead of a direct call, matching how function parameter variants are already handled.

## How Has This Been Tested?

- New test: `opaque_behavioral_predicates.move` covering simple opaque, recursive opaque (bounded), and mutually recursive opaque functions with behavioral predicates. All verify cleanly.
- Full prover test suite passes (173 tests, 0 failures).

## Type of Change
- [x] Bug fix

## Which Components or Systems Does This Change Impact?
- [x] Move/Aptos Virtual Machine

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

<!-- CURSOR_SUMMARY -->
---

> [!NOTE]
> **Medium Risk**
> Changes core Boogie codegen for closure dispatch and result/mutation handling, which could affect prover soundness/precision across many specs, though the change is localized and covered by a new regression test.
> 
> **Overview**
> Fixes Boogie generation in `bytecode_translator.rs` so `$apply` no longer emits a direct `call` when a closure target is an *opaque* Move function (which has no Boogie procedure body); instead it models the call using behavioral predicates (`aborts_of`/`result_of`) and shared result/abort/memory-havoc logic.
> 
> Refactors the behavioral-predicate call emission into a new helper (`emit_behavioral_predicate_body`) and adds a new functional test `opaque_behavioral_predicates.move` covering simple, recursive, and mutually-recursive opaque functions using `result_of`/`aborts_of`.
>

title: pod: refactor executor creation to enforce timeouts consistently
body:
This commit refactors the executor creation logic to use a single shared helper function getExecutorFromRequest() that properly enforces timeouts during connection establishment, not just during streaming.

Changes:
- Add getExecutorFromRequest() helper with configurable timeouts for:
  - TCP connection establishment (DialTimeout)
  - TLS handshake (TLSHandshakeTimeout)
  - Response headers (ResponseHeaderTimeout)
- All methods now use WebSocket/SPDY fallback executor pattern
- All methods now set PingPeriod=0 to avoid large file transfer issues (kubernetes/kubernetes#60140)

Fixed bug in ExecCommandWithTimeout:
- Previously only enforced timeout during streaming phase
- Now enforces timeout during connection establishment phase as well
- Uses default timeouts: 30s dial, 10s TLS, 30s response headers

Integration tests:
- Added 6 integration tests to validate timeout enforcement
- Tests validate connection-level timeout handling (critical bug fix)
- Tests validate PingPeriod=0 configuration for large file transfers
- Discovered 64KB buffer limit in Copy method (separate from PingPeriod)

The tests validate that the refactored getExecutorFromRequest() helper
correctly enforces timeouts during TCP connection, TLS handshake, and
response header phases, not just during command streaming.

Run tests: `go test -tags=integration ./integration -run TestPod -v`

Benefits:
- Eliminates code duplication across ExecCommand, ExecCommandWithTimeout, Copy
- Consistent timeout enforcement across all exec methods
- All methods benefit from WebSocket/SPDY fallback reliability
- Flexible design: pass 0 for timeouts to disable enforcement

Commands now use `@work(thread=True)` async workers with immediate toast feedback instead of intrusive streaming modals, providing better UX. Also extracts `_extract_subprocess_error()` helper to consolidate error handling (was duplicated 8x), removes `_push_streaming_detail` helper, and converts detail screen handlers to dismiss-and-delegate pattern. ## Files Changed

Implementation Plan (Issue #8047) # Convert TUI commands from modal to toast (fire-and-forget) ## Context The `address_remote` and `close_plan` commands were recently converted from modal-based (PlanDetailScreen push with streaming output) to toast-based (fire-and-forget with `@work(thread=True)` background workers). self._check_objective_async(row.plan_id) ``` **one_shot_plan** (lines 1023-1036): Replace with: ```python elif command_id == "one_shot_plan": self.notify(f"Dispatching one-shot plan for objective #{row.plan_id}...") self._one_shot_plan_async(row.plan_id) ``` ### Step 3: Convert detail screen to dismiss-and-delegate In `plan_detail_screen.py execute_command()`, convert these handlers to the dismiss-and-delegate pattern (matching `close_plan` at line 715): **fix_conflicts_remote** (lines 699-705): ```python elif command_id == "fix_conflicts_remote": if row.pr_number is not None: self.dismiss() if isinstance(self.app, ErkDashApp): self.app.notify(f"Dispatching fix-conflicts for PR #{row.pr_number}...") self.app._fix_conflicts_remote_async(row.pr_number) ``` **address_remote** (lines 707-713): Already toast at app level, convert detail screen to match: ```python elif command_id == "address_remote": if row.pr_number is not None: self.dismiss() if isinstance(self.app, ErkDashApp): self.app.notify(f"Dispatching address for PR #{row.pr_number}...") self.app._address_remote_async(row.pr_number) ``` **submit_to_queue** (lines 724-732): ```python elif command_id == "submit_to_queue": if row.plan_url: self.dismiss() if isinstance(self.app, ErkDashApp): self.app.notify(f"Submitting plan #{row.plan_id} to queue...") self.app._submit_to_queue_async(row.plan_id) ``` **land_pr** (lines 734-771): ```python elif command_id == "land_pr": if row.pr_number and row.pr_head_branch: self.dismiss() if isinstance(self.app, ErkDashApp): self.app.notify(f"Landing PR #{row.pr_number}...") self.app._land_pr_async(row.pr_number, row.pr_head_branch, row.objective_issue) ``` Also update `action_fix_conflicts_remote` (line 367-375) to use dismiss-and-delegate. ### Step 4: Remove `_push_streaming_detail` helper and `RealCommandExecutor` import in `fix_conflicts_remote` With all callers converted, delete `_push_streaming_detail` (lines 563-598). Also remove the `RealCommandExecutor` construction in the `fix_conflicts_remote` app-level handler (lines 912-919) since it's no longer needed. ### Step 5: Update tests in `test_app.py` **Replace modal-push tests with async subprocess tests** following the `_address_remote_async` test pattern (lines 2230-2327): For each converted command, add tests that: 1. Monkeypatch `subprocess.run` to capture args 2. Call `app._command_async(args)` 3. `await pilot.pause(0.3)` for background thread 4. Assert captured command args 5. Assert refresh triggered on success 6. Assert no refresh on CalledProcessError **Update guard condition tests** (no PR, no branch, etc.) to assert no screen push (these stay similar but now assert no toast/no worker call). **Remove tests that assert `PlanDetailScreen` push** for converted commands. ### Step 6: Update tests in `test_execute_command.py` - Convert `_CapturingPlanDetailScreen` tests for land_pr to dismiss-and-delegate assertions - Remove `_CapturingPlanDetailScreen` if no more callers (used only by land_pr tests) - Update fix_conflicts_remote and submit_to_queue guard tests (remove repo_root checks since dismiss-and-delegate doesn't need it in detail screen) ## Verification 1. Run TUI tests: `uv run pytest tests/tui/ -x` 2. Run type checker: `uv run ty check src/erk/tui/app.py src/erk/tui/screens/plan_detail_screen.py` 3. Manual smoke test: `erk dash -i`, select a plan with PR, trigger fix-conflicts/land/submit from both command palette and detail screen - should see toasts instead of modals

Creating these docs would document explicitly rejected patterns and mislead future agents. ### Low-value items from #8046 - Item 5 (bot review lifecycle): Informational only, not actionable - Item 6 (test coverage expectations): Informational only - Item 7 (inline test import exception): Too granular for docs - Item 8 (bot pattern exception enforcement): Informational about tooling - Item 10 (column width evolution): Width unchanged, no doc needed - Item 11 (fetch_count docstring): Better as code comment, not learned doc ### Low-value items from #8044 - Item 5 (emoji column width calculation): Already correct at 7, no doc needed - Item 7 (ready-to-land terminology): Low value terminology update ## Attribution Items by source: - **#8062**: Steps 4, 13 (partial) - **#8058**: None (all discarded) - **#8056**: Steps 7, 8, 9, 12, 15 - **#8052**: Steps 10, 11, 16 - **#8051**: Step 10 (merged into #8052's items) - **#8046**: Steps 1 (partial), 3 (partial), 6, 14 - **#8044**: Steps 1 (partial), 2, 3, 5, 13 (partial) ## Verification 1. All stale doc references resolved (Steps 1-3) 2. New docs created with correct frontmatter and category placement (Steps 4-9) 3. Existing docs updated with new sections (Steps 10-12) 4. title: fix: Avoid webhook feedback loop on no-ops comment events
body: Added logic to prevent errors from no-ops comment events from creating a feedback loop. When a "no-ops" comment event occurs, the MetadataResolve error is now skipped, preventing the creation of a comment that would re-trigger the same event. The test case for Gitea was updated to account for this change. It now waits for a short period to allow any potential feedback loops to settle and then asserts that only a single failure comment was posted, rather than multiple duplicates. For whatever reasons i could not reproduce this behaviour with github webhook and gitlab, only on forgejo ## Screenshots ### Before ### After ## 📝 Description of the Change When a no-ops comment event occurs (e.g., a comment that matches no pipeline configurations), the system was reporting a MetadataResolve error by posting a failure comment. That comment triggered another webhook event, which hit the same error, creating an unbounded feedback loop of duplicate failure comments. The fix adds a targeted check in `pkg/pipelineascode/match.go`: if the current event is a no-ops comment event, MetadataResolve errors are silently logged instead of surfaced as failure comments. All other event types retain the existing error-reporting behaviour. ## 👨🏻‍ Linked Jira https://issues.redhat.com/browse/SRVKP-10912 ## 🔗 Linked GitHub Issue Fixes # ## 🚀 Type of Change - [x] 🐛 Bug fix (`fix:`) - [ ] ✨ New feature (`feat:`) - [ ] 💥 Breaking change (`feat!:`, `fix!:`) - [ ] 📚 Documentation update (`docs:`) - [ ] ⚙️ Chore (`chore:`) - [ ] 💅 Refactor (`refactor:`) - [ ] 🔧 Enhancement (`enhance:`) - [ ] 📦 Dependency update (`deps:`) ## 🧪 Testing Strategy - [x] Unit tests - [ ] Integration tests - [x] End-to-end tests - [ ] Manual testing - [ ] Not Applicable ## 🤖 AI Assistance - [ ] I have not used any AI assistance for this PR. - [x] I have used AI assistance for this PR. title: Major refactor to use pluggable modules for inference runtimes
body:
Related to #2379 

Inference runtime logic is spread throughout the code-base, particularly in `cli.py` and `transport/base.py`. Much of the functionality is runtime specific, in many cases the entire subcommand is llama.cpp specific.

The data-driven inference spec approach is too rigid to represent this. This change attempts to switch to a python plugin approach instead, and refactor the runtime specific logic out of the core modules and into the relevant runtime plugin. This leaves the core modules to primarily handle managing the models and interacting with the container engine

There is still more work to be done, e.g I've largely ignored the daemon. Convert handling in the oci transport needs further refactoring.

However this is already a very significant refactor so I'm creating a draft PR for review/feedback on the general approach.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

# Summary from Claude

plugins: replace YAML+Jinja2 inference specs with Python runtime plugin system

Replaces the YAML+Jinja2 inference specification system with a Python plugin architecture for runtime inference engine management.

## Plugin system

Each inference engine is now a self-contained Python plugin discovered via importlib entry points (group: "ramalama.runtimes"):

- ramalama/plugins/interface.py — RuntimePlugin(ABC) and InferenceRuntimePlugin(ABC): the public contracts every plugin must satisfy.  InferenceRuntimePlugin adds @abstractmethod _cmd_run and three inference-specific hooks (chat_prefix, handle_nocontainer_chat, api_model_name).
- ramalama/plugins/runtimes/common.py — BaseInferenceRuntime: concrete base with run/serve subcommand registration and _run_handler / _serve_handler.  ContainerizedInferenceRuntimePlugin extends it with the RAG subcommand (only registered when containers are enabled).
- ramalama/plugins/runtimes/llama_cpp_commands.py — LlamaCppCommands mixin: pure command builders (_cmd_run, _cmd_serve, _cmd_perplexity, _cmd_bench, _cmd_rag, _cmd_run_rag, _cmd_serve_rag, _cmd_convert, _cmd_quantize) plus llama.cpp-specific constants and _default_threads.
- ramalama/plugins/runtimes/llama_cpp.py — LlamaCppPlugin inherits from LlamaCppCommands + ContainerizedInferenceRuntimePlugin; owns image selection, health checks, argparse registration, container operations, and command handlers for bench/perplexity/convert/benchmarks.
- ramalama/plugins/runtimes/vllm.py — VllmPlugin: vLLM support with its own image defaults and max_model_len arg.
- ramalama/plugins/runtimes/mlx.py — MlxPlugin: macOS/Apple Silicon only, always --nocontainer.  Implements all four inference hooks to keep MLX-specific behaviour out of shared code.
- ramalama/plugins/loader.py — get_runtime(name), get_all_runtimes(), assemble_command() via PluginRegistry.
- ramalama/plugins/registry.py — generic PluginRegistry[T] backed by entry points.

## Class hierarchy

```
  RuntimePlugin (ABC)                      interface.py
    └── InferenceRuntimePlugin (ABC)       interface.py
          └── BaseInferenceRuntime         runtimes/common.py
                └── ContainerizedInferenceRuntimePlugin
                      └── LlamaCppPlugin (+ LlamaCppCommands mixin)
                      └── VllmPlugin
                └── MlxPlugin
```
## Transport changes

- bench, perplexity, serve, run subcommand handlers moved from Transport base class into plugin _*_handler methods.
- _convert_to_gguf and _quantize moved from Transport into LlamaCppPlugin.
- Runtime-specific CLI args (--ngl, --threads, --cache-reuse, --thinking, --webui, --model-draft, --max-model-len) moved into each plugin's _add_*_inference_args and register_subcommands methods.
- MLX-specific branches (== "mlx" checks) removed from chat.py and transports/base.py; behaviour now lives in MlxPlugin hooks.
- build_command dispatches via _cmd_<subcommand> naming convention.

## Config changes

- RamalamaImages and RamalamaRagImages dataclasses removed from config.py; images and rag_images are now plain dict[str, str]. Plugin-owned image defaults live in each plugin (LlamaCppPlugin, VllmPlugin); RAG image defaults live in rag.py (_DEFAULT_RAG_IMAGES).
- accel_image() bug fixed: plugin delegation now only runs when no explicit images= dict is passed (previously it ran even for RAG image selection, returning the wrong image).
- stack.py: fix args.context → args.ctx_size in _gen_llama_args.

## arg_types cleanup

- ServeRunArgsType removed: it was a monolithic Protocol listing every possible serve/run field across all runtimes, with stale field names and llama.cpp-specific fields.  RagArgsType now extends DefaultArgsType directly with only the fields RAG actually needs.

## Removed

- inference-spec/ directory (YAML specs + JSON schema)
- ramalama/command/ directory (factory, context, schema, error)
- test/unit/command/ directory

title: TheWire: Fix UI
body:
# Resolve UI Freezes in "The Wire" feature
## Description
This PR addresses recurring UI freezes associated with "The Wire" feature. The root cause was identified as synchronous, blocking calls to the background service (`rsWire->getGroups`) performed on the main UI thread. These freezes occurred not only when viewing the "The Wire" tab but also in the background due to event handlers triggering data refreshes upon receiving network updates.
## Changes
- **Asynchronous Data Loading**: Refactored `WireDialog::requestGroupData` to use `RsThread::async` for fetching group data in a background thread.
- **Thread-Safe UI Updates**: Used `RsQThreadUtils::postToObject` to safely update the UI components from the background thread once data is available.
- **Non-Blocking Actions**: Converted [subscribe], [unsubscribe], and [PVHfollow] methods to be asynchronous to prevent minor stalls during user interactions.
- **Code Optimization**: Updated `WireDialog::updateGroups` to take a `const std::vector<RsWireGroup>&` to improve efficiency and ensure compatibility with the new asynchronous patterns.

title: [storage] Update hot state deferred merge metrics more frequently
body:
The `hot_state_deferred_merge_old_views` and `hot_state_deferred_merge_version_lag`
gauges were only updated at the end of each `run` iteration, right after publishing
a new commit. At that point `try_merge` typically fails because readers still hold
the previous view, so the metrics always showed `old_views=1` and a non-zero version
lag (equal to the batch size), even though the merge succeeds a few milliseconds
later in `next_to_commit`'s timeout retry loop.

- Extract `update_deferred_merge_gauges()` helper to avoid duplication.
- Call it in `try_merge` on both the success path (lag=0 after merge) and the
  `return false` path (accurate lag while blocked by lingering readers).
- Move `hot_state_items` / `hot_state_key_bytes` / `hot_state_value_bytes` gauges
  into `apply_delta_to_base`, since that's the only place that mutates DashMaps
  and byte counters.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

<!-- CURSOR_SUMMARY -->
---

> [!NOTE]
> **Low Risk**
> Metrics-only refactor with minor control-flow changes around when gauges are updated; no changes to merge logic or persisted data paths.
> 
> **Overview**
> Improves hot-state deferred-merge observability by updating `hot_state_deferred_merge_old_views` and `hot_state_deferred_merge_version_lag` inside `try_merge()` on both blocked and success paths, rather than only after publishing a new commit.
> 
> Refactors metric updates by extracting `update_deferred_merge_gauges()` and moving `hot_state_items`/`hot_state_key_bytes`/`hot_state_value_bytes` gauge updates into `apply_delta_to_base()` (the only place that mutates the DashMaps/byte counters).

title: Hierarchical symbol impact analysis for pytest marker analyzer
body:
## Summary

Adds member-level and function-call narrowing to the pytest marker analyzer to dramatically reduce false positives when determining which marked tests should run based on PR changes.

### Problem

The analyzer treated entire classes as single symbols. When ANY method inside a ~1000-line class like `VirtualMachineForTests` changed, ALL tests importing the class were flagged as affected — even tests that never use the modified methods. PR #3847 reported 83 affected smoke tests when the actual count was 2.

### Solution

Three layers of narrowing:

1. **Member-level narrowing** — Maps changed lines to specific class members (methods/properties). Builds an intra-class call graph to expand transitively (if method A calls self.B() and B is modified, A is also affected).

2. **Function-call narrowing** — Checks if the specific test function actually calls the modified top-level functions, rather than relying on file-level imports. This handles the case where a test file imports `assert_vm_xml_efi` for sibling test functions, but the smoke tests in the same file never call it.

3. **Test file self-modification** — When a test's own file is changed, applies symbol analysis instead of blindly flagging. Only flags the test if its own function/class was modified.

### Additional improvements

- **Remote mode fix**: Fetches the PR's HEAD version of files via GitHub API so diff line numbers align correctly with symbol maps (previously used local file which could be on a different branch)
- **Code deduplication**: Consolidated 4 duplicate code paths (`_is_fixture_decorator`, `_parse_diff_for_functions`, `_get_modified_function_names`, `extract_modified_items`) into standalone module-level functions
- **Extracted helpers**: `_parse_test_name()`, `_find_test_function_node()` to eliminate repeated patterns

### Results

| PR | Before | After | Reduction |
|----|--------|-------|-----------|
| #3847 (VirtualMachineForTests refactor, 69 files) | 83/83 smoke tests | 2/83 smoke tests | **97.6%** |

The 2 remaining tests (`test_migrate_vm` variants) genuinely call `validate_libvirt_persistent_domain` whose signature changed.

### Safety invariants preserved

| Scenario | Behavior |
|---|---|
| Changed line inside class but outside any method | Class-level fallback (flags all importers) |
| `getattr()`/`setattr()`/`delattr()` in test body | Returns None → no narrowing (conservative) |
| Method removed from class | Included as modified → tests flagged |
| Test imports class but usage can't be determined | No narrowing (conservative) |
| `__init__` modified + test constructs class | Always included in accessed attrs |
| Remote mode API fetch fails | Returns None → file-level fallback |

Co-authored-by: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

## Test plan

- [x] `pre-commit run --all-files` — all 15 hooks pass
- [x] `tox -e utilities-unittests` — 671 tests pass, 98.61% coverage
- [x] `uv run pytest scripts/tests_analyzer/tests/ -v` — 56 new tests pass
- [x] Validated against PR #3847: 83→2 affected smoke tests
- [x] Validated against 9 mismatch PRs from CodeRabbit comparison
- [x] Code review loop: 3 reviewers, all findings addressed

title: Persnickety tidiness: tag renumbering, ExplicitTagStorable, native tag utilities
body:
## Summary

A batch of tidiness improvements to the storable/canonical-hash module: tag renumbering for conceptual clarity, a new base class to reduce duplication, and native-value tag dispatch utilities.

### Commits

1. **Renumber primitive tag bytes by conceptual size** (`a1509105d`) -- Reorder canonical hash tags from `null`(0x20) through `epoch_days`(0x28) in smallest-to-biggest conceptual order. Affects `canonical-hash.ts`, both spec files, and all hash test expectations.

2. **Extract `ExplicitTagStorable` base class** (`91ad4e4ad`) -- New abstract base for `UnknownStorable` and `ProblematicStorable`, collapsing duplicate `instanceof` checks in `json-encoding.ts` and `type-handlers.ts`. Spec Section 3 updated with new subsection.

3. **Add `NATIVE_TAGS` + `tagFromNativeValue`/`tagFromNativeClass` utilities** (`baff093dc`) -- Canonical constructor-switch dispatch for mapping native JS values to their storable tags. Error subclass switch (`TypeError`, `RangeError`, etc.) with `Error.isError()` fallback. Refactored `isConvertibleNativeInstance()` and `toRichStorableValue()`.

4. **Add `Error.isError()` fallback to `tagFromNativeClass()`** (`af0ce96c1`) -- Mirror the `Error.isError()` prototype check from `tagFromNativeValue()` in the class-level utility for consistency.

5. **Add tests for `tagFromNativeValue`/`tagFromNativeClass` exotic Error cases** (`4c3730228`) -- Test coverage for the `Error.isError()` fallback paths in both utilities.

title: refactor: Redesign index.toon with L2+title hint strategy (#88)

## Summary

Redesigned `index.toon` to use L2+title hints instead of L1+L2+L3, reducing file selection noise by 73% (6.0 → 1.6 files average) while maintaining 100% accuracy. **All 93 entries have been migrated** to the new format.

**Related Issue**: Closes #88

## Changes

### Core Changes
- **Modified**: `.claude/skills/nabledge-6/knowledge/index.toon` - All 93 entries updated to L2+title format
- **Modified**: `.claude/skills/nabledge-test/scenarios/nabledge-6/scenarios.json` - Added 4 code-analysis scenarios (now 10 total)
- **Removed**: `.claude/skills/skill-creator/references/scoring-schema.json` - Unused duplicate file

### What Changed

**Removed**:
- L1 generic domain terms: データベース, ファイル, ハンドラ, バッチ (caused noise matches)
- L3 functional keywords: ページング, 検索, 登録, 更新 (moved to .index sections)

**Added**:
- Entry titles in Japanese: ユニバーサルDAO, データバインド, トランザクション管理ハンドラ
- Entry titles in English: UniversalDao, DataBind, TransactionManagementHandler
- Class names: DbConnectionManagementHandler, FilePathSetting

**Kept**:

1. **Analysis**: Identified L1 keywords as root cause of noise matches (generic terms matched 5-15 files)
2. **Design**: Separated file-level (L2+title) from section-level (L3) hint strategy

title: [node] Name all unnamed threads on the aptos-node path
body:
Unnamed threads make debugging harder — stack traces, `/proc/<pid>/task/*/comm`,
and logging all show unhelpful default names. This names all `thread::spawn` calls
on the aptos-node execution path using `thread::Builder::new().name(...)`.

Changes:
- `rocksdb_property_reporter.rs`: named `"rocksdb-prop"`
- `db_indexer.rs`: named `"db-idx-commit"`
- `aptos_logger.rs`: named `"logger-svc"`
- `safety-rules/thread.rs`: named `"safety-rules"`
- `inspection-service/server/mod.rs`: refactored from `thread::spawn` +
  `runtime.block_on()` to `runtime.spawn()`, returning the `Runtime` to the
  caller (stored in `AptosHandle`), consistent with every other service in
  aptos-node. This eliminates the unnamed thread entirely.

All names are ≤15 chars to fit the Linux `comm` limit.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

<!-- CURSOR_SUMMARY -->
---

> [!NOTE]
> **Low Risk**
> Mostly operational/debuggability changes (thread naming and runtime ownership) with minimal impact on business logic; primary risk is inadvertently changing inspection service lifecycle/shutdown behavior.
> 
> **Overview**
> Ensures background threads on the `aptos-node` execution path have stable, human-readable names to improve debuggability in stack traces and `/proc`.
> 
> Replaces several `thread::spawn` calls with `thread::Builder::name(...).spawn(...)` (logger service, safety rules thread service, RocksDB property reporter, and internal indexer DB committer). The node inspection service is refactored to run via `runtime.spawn()` and now returns its `Runtime`, which is stored in `AptosHandle` to keep the service alive instead of relying on an unnamed OS thread.
>

title: refactor: separate container backend from agent runtime (two axes)
body:
## Summary

Separates the backend system into two orthogonal axes and implements the OpenCode agent runtime:

• *BackendType* (`apple-container` | `docker`) — how we isolate the agent (the container)
• *AgentRuntime* (`claude-agent-sdk` | `opencode`) — what runs inside the container

Previously these were smashed into one `BackendType` enum. Now you can mix and match:
- Claude Agent SDK in Apple Container (current default)
- Claude Agent SDK in Docker
- OpenCode in Apple Container
- OpenCode in Docker

Agents are always containerized — there's no "none" option for container.

## Upgrade safety

**Existing setups are unaffected.** All defaults are preserved:
- `agentRuntime` defaults to `claude-agent-sdk` — no behavior change unless you opt in
- DB migrations are additive (new columns/tables with safe defaults, no drops)
- `auto-update.sh` handles everything: pull → build host → rebuild container → restart

**auto-update will not break your setup when this merges.** The opencode CLI is installed in a Docker layer *before* the `COPY` steps, so it hits the build cache on subsequent updates (no re-download after first build). If the build fails for any reason, `set -e` exits before the service restarts — your running instance stays up safely.

### Manual upgrade path

If you're upgrading manually or something seems off after auto-update:

```bash
# 1. Bust container build cache (Apple Container only — buildkit caches COPY steps)
container builder stop && container builder rm && container builder start
./container/build.sh

# 2. Verify DB schema
./.claude/skills/setup/scripts/02b-init-db.sh

# 3. Restart
launchctl kickstart -k gui/$(id -u)/com.omniclaw
```

Full upgrade guide is in the `/setup` skill under **Upgrading OmniClaw**.

### Rollback

DB changes are additive — rolling back the code after migration is safe. Extra columns/tables are harmless on older code.

## What changed

### Runtime separation (first commit)
• Added `AgentRuntime` type and `agentRuntime` field to `Agent`, `RegisteredGroup`, `ContainerInput`
• Added `agent_runtime` column to DB schema (defaults to `claude-agent-sdk`)
• `agentRuntime` flows from config → DB → agent registry → ContainerInput → agent-runner
• Agent-runner dispatches on `agentRuntime`
• Removed `isLocal` field, dead `getCloudAgentIds()`, standalone `OpenCodeBackend` class
• Removed `OPENCODE_PORT_BASE`/`OPENCODE_MODEL` from host config (container-side concern)

### OpenCode runtime adapter (second commit)
• `container/agent-runner/src/opencode-runtime.ts` — full runtime implementation:
  - Starts `opencode serve` as a background process inside the container
  - Connects via `@opencode-ai/sdk` client
  - Creates/resumes sessions, sends prompts, extracts responses
  - Same IPC protocol as Claude SDK runtime (stdout markers, file-based message polling, `_close` sentinel)
  - Injects CLAUDE.md system context via no-reply setup prompt
  - Supports scheduled tasks, multi-channel routing, follow-up messages
• `container/agent-runner/src/index.ts` — dispatches to opencode runtime when `agentRuntime === 'opencode'`
• `container/agent-runner/package.json` — added `@opencode-ai/sdk` dependency
• `container/Dockerfile` — installs `opencode` CLI via official install script
• `container/agent-runner/src/__tests__/opencode-runtime.test.ts` — 13 tests for IPC protocol, output format, type safety

### Bug fixes (in-branch)
• Fixed OpenCode auth isolation — copy only `auth.json`/`mcp-auth.json` into per-group dir instead of mounting the entire `~/.local/share/opencode` directory (prevented host DB corruption from concurrent container writes)
• Wired OmniClaw MCP tools (`send_message`, `react_to_message`, `schedule_task`, etc.) into OpenCode sessions via `OPENCODE_CONFIG_CONTENT` MCP config
• Fixed double-send when agent calls `send_message` as final response — tool description and return value now tell the agent to wrap remaining text in `<internal>` tags
• Fixed typing indicator using wrong Discord bot when piping messages to active containers

## Test plan

- [x] `tsc --noEmit` passes (0 errors)
- [x] `bun run build` passes
- [x] All 545 tests pass (0 failures)
- [x] 13 new OpenCode runtime tests pass
- [x] Zero breaking changes — all defaults preserved
- [ ] End-to-end: register a group with `agentRuntime: 'opencode'` and verify it responds

title: [rocr-debug-agent] Refactor and modernize build system
body:
This change is comprised of 5 commits:

### Commit 1
[Remove CPack packaging directives from CMakeLists.txt](https://github.com/ROCm/rocm-systems/commit/0b169d96e2f33f8e2ce8a17851c122f05de678c6) 

Removes all CPack-related configuration including RPM and Debian package
settings, component definitions, and packaging logic.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

### Commit 2
[Modernize CMakeLists.txt dependency discovery](https://github.com/ROCm/rocm-systems/commit/c74454c10381f0f6262add005366427433489cd9) 

Refactor dependency discovery to use modern CMake practices:
- Remove custom Find modules (FindLibDw, FindLibElf, FindROCR)
- Use standard find_package, find_path, and find_library
- Always provide imported targets for consistent usage
- Try CONFIG mode first (TheRock), fallback to manual discovery (standalone)
- Use target-based linking instead of custom include/library variables
- Remove cmake/modules directory as it's no longer needed

This eliminates code duplication between TheRock and standalone builds
while maintaining compatibility with both environments.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

### Commit 3
[Further modernize CMakeLists.txt](https://github.com/ROCm/rocm-systems/commit/ed07c6714d8d1d0b98aae89da0ed6f717a65d839) 

Apply modern CMake best practices:
- Bump minimum version to 3.21 (enables target_link_options)
- Specify LANGUAGES explicitly in project()
- Replace file(GLOB) with explicit target_sources()
- Use target_link_options() instead of embedding linker flags
- Consolidate target_compile_options() into single call
- Use generator expression for HAVE_MEMFD_CREATE definition
- Consolidate install(TARGETS) commands for both components
- Improve readability with better formatting

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

### Commit 4
[Modernize test/CMakeLists.txt](https://github.com/ROCm/rocm-systems/commit/bdf133f3979f903b8514a202190c85b311c676c9) 

Apply modern CMake 3.21+ practices for HIP language support:
- Bump minimum version to 3.21 (native HIP language support)
- Enable HIP as a first-class language in project()
- Remove deprecated FindHIP MODULE usage and hip_add_* commands
- Use standard add_executable/add_library with LANGUAGE HIP property
- Find HIP using CONFIG mode instead of MODULE mode
- Use CMAKE_HIP_ARCHITECTURES instead of GPU_TARGETS
- Remove file(GLOB) and explicitly list headers
- Use GNUInstallDirs for standard install paths
- Install tests to tests/rocm-debug-agent instead of src/ and make them
  runnable from both the build and install tree
- Remove CMakeLists.txt and unneeded files from installation
- Remove unused NODEBUG_OBJ variable
- Remove deprecated HIP_SOURCE_PROPERTY_FORMAT property
- Use modern target-based compile options
- Use amdclang++/clang++ instead of hipcc and adjust its build
  optimization level for hipfb files to match hipcc

This reduces code duplication and simplifies maintenance while
ensuring compatibility with modern ROCm/HIP toolchains.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

### Commit 5
[Reformat CMakeLists.txt with pre-commit hook](https://github.com/ROCm/rocm-systems/commit/9fc2ea31f604a18433d089d09199ac571e02aaf6) 

Make sure our cmake files adhere to the formatting the pre-commit hook in
TheRock expects.

### notes

This change needs to be merged alongside a TheRock change to use the new debug agent paths for testing. That's why the testing job fails. But we're dealing with a submodule change and a TheRock change, and those can't be made atomically at the moment.

title: Release 21.0.0
body:
## Release 21.0.0

This release includes breaking changes, new features, and refactoring improvements across the design system packages.

### 📦 Package Versions

- `@metamask/design-system-react`: **0.9.0**
- `@metamask/design-system-react-native`: **0.8.0**
- `@metamask/design-system-shared`: **0.2.0**

### ⚠️ Breaking Changes

#### BadgeStatus Migration to String Union Types (#912)

The `BadgeStatus` component has been migrated from TypeScript enums to string union types with const objects, implementing ADR-0003 and ADR-0004:

**What Changed:**
- `BadgeStatusStatus` and `BadgeStatusSize` enums replaced with const objects
- Types now centralized in `@metamask/design-system-shared` package
- Enables structural typing - both const values and string literals now accepted

**Migration Required:**
- ✅ **Recommended**: Import from shared package
  ```typescript
  import { BadgeStatusStatus } from '@metamask/design-system-shared'
  ```
- ⚠️ **Still works** but deprecated: Importing from component packages will re-export from shared
- Const object values remain the same: `BadgeStatusStatus.Active` still works
- String literals now supported: `'active'` accepted where `BadgeStatusStatus.Active` is expected

**Affected Packages:**
- `@metamask/design-system-react@0.9.0`
- `@metamask/design-system-react-native@0.8.0`
- `@metamask/design-system-shared@0.2.0`

### ✨ New Features

#### RadioButton Component (#926)
- Added `RadioButton` component to React Native
- Supports checked, disabled, read-only, and danger states
- Full accessibility support with `role="radio"` and `accessibilityState`
- Optional label rendering

### 🔨 Refactoring

#### BottomSheetFooter Reorganization (#933)
- Moved `BottomSheetFooter` from `BottomSheets/BottomSheetFooter/` to `BottomSheetFooter/`
- Updated import paths and Storybook title
- No breaking changes - all package imports continue to work

### 📚 References

- ADR-0003: Enum to String Union Migration
- ADR-0004: Centralized Types Architecture
- [MetaMask Decisions Repository](https://github.com/MetaMask/decisions)

### ✅ Checklist

- [x] All changelogs updated with human-readable descriptions
- [x] Breaking changes clearly documented
- [x] Changelog validation passed (`yarn changelog:validate`)
- [x] All packages built successfully

Risk is moderated by extensive baseline test updates, but the surface area spans MCP server, plugin generation, and Move prover internals.
> 
> **Overview**
> Adds a new MCP tool, `move_package_spec_infer`, which runs the Move Prover’s WP inference pipeline and **injects inferred specs back into package source files**, followed by the same formatting/AST checks used by the edit hook.

title: refactor: move secret creation to reconciler
body:
Move git-auth secret creation from startPR (controller) to the reconciler via a new `secret-created` annotation. PipelineRuns are now created with secret-created=false, and the reconciler creates the secret and patches the annotation to true before the PipelineRun proceeds.

This decouples secret lifecycle management from the controller path, enabling the reconciler to handle retries and error recovery. RBAC is updated accordingly: secret create/update permissions move from the controller role to the watcher role.

title: [storage] Key state pipeline by HashValue for hot state KV persistence
body:
Refactor the state tracking pipeline so that `LayeredMap`, `StateDelta`,
`HotStateLRU`, `HotStateView`, and `HotStateBase` DashMaps are all keyed
by `HashValue` instead of `StateKey`. This enables reconstructing the hot
state LRU linked lists from the on-disk `hot_state_kv_db` on node startup,
since the DB schema only stores `HashValue` keys.

**`StateSlot`** becomes a struct wrapping `(StateKey, StateSlotKind)` so
that the `StateKey` travels with the value through the hash-keyed maps.
`StateSlotKind` is the old enum with variants `ColdVacant`, `HotVacant`,
`ColdOccupied`, `HotOccupied`. All existing methods delegate to the inner
kind, and `maybe_update_jmt` now uses the embedded key (no parameter).

**LRU pointers** (`LRUEntry`) change from `StateKey` to `HashValue`, so
`prev`/`next` in the doubly-linked list, `head`/`tail` in
`HotStateMetadata`, and `HotStateLRU.pending` are all `HashValue`-keyed.
This is the core change that makes DB-based LRU reconstruction possible.

**`HotStateView`** trait method changes from `get_state_slot(&StateKey)`
to `get_state_slot(&HashValue)`. The `LayeredHotStateView` (RCU layer in
aptosdb) does delta and DashMap lookups directly by hash.

**`HotStateShardUpdates`** insertions/evictions change from
`HashMap<StateKey, _>` to `HashMap<HashValue, _>`, removing redundant
hashing in the DB write path.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

<!-- CURSOR_SUMMARY -->
---

> [!NOTE]
> **High Risk**
> High risk because it refactors core state storage/hot-state caching and commit paths (LRU pointers, delta overlays, DB persistence) to new keying/slot representations, which could cause subtle state read/write or eviction inconsistencies if any call site is missed.
> 
> **Overview**
> Refactors the hot-state pipeline to be keyed by `HashValue` end-to-end (including `LayeredMap` shards, `StateDelta`, `HotStateLRU`, `HotStateView`, and AptosDB hot-state DashMaps), aligning in-memory structures with the on-disk hot-state KV schema and removing repeated hashing in persistence paths.
> 
> Changes `StateSlot` from an enum into a struct embedding the full `StateKey` plus a new `StateSlotKind` (the old variants), updates constructors/helpers (e.g., `from_db_get`, `maybe_update_jmt`) and updates callers/tests across storage, simulation, replay, validator interface, and resource viewer to pass/propagate the key and use the new `kind()`/`state_key()` APIs.
> 
> <sup>Written by [Cursor Bugbot](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot) for commit 3e3128878b3282188de3c3011415826e22da9ab0. This will update automatically on new commits. Configure [here](https://cursor.com/dashboard?tab=bugbot).</sup>
<!-- /CURSOR_SUMMARY -->

title: apollo_dashboard,apollo_deployments: add top-level intervalSec to Alerts; use it in alert builder
body:
Move the evaluation interval from a per-alert field to a top-level field
on the Alerts struct. The interval is group-scoped in Grafana, so a single
value for all alerts is the correct model.

Also restructure alert_builder.py to upload/dump per rule-group instead of
per individual alert, using the top-level intervalSec as the group interval.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

<!-- CURSOR_SUMMARY -->
---

> [!NOTE]
> **Medium Risk**
> Changes the alert JSON schema and the provisioning/deployment flow (grouped updates and per-group CRDs), which could alter alert evaluation cadence or cause missing/duplicated rules if grouping or intervals are misapplied.
> 
> **Overview**
> Updates the generated `dev_grafana_alerts.json` schema to include top-level `groups` (with per-group evaluation intervals) and moves alerts into these rule groups (`evaluation_rate_high/default/low`), along with various severity/observer applicability tweaks driven by the regrouping.
> 
> Replaces `AlertGroup` with `EvaluationRate` in the Rust alert definitions, adds deterministic sorting, and changes `Alerts` serialization to emit the new `groups + alerts` structure.
> 
> Refactors `alert_builder.py` and the sequencer Grafana construct to provision alerts **per rule group** (one group file/CRD per group) and to set the rule-group interval once, rather than per alert.
>

title: Add is_disabled() to CircuitEval trait and use it consistently.
body:
Introduces an `is_disabled()` method on `CircuitEval` (based on
interaction_columns == 0) and replaces ad-hoc checks with it across
proof config construction, composition polynomial evaluation, and
builtin verification. Removes the ProofConfig dependency from
verify_builtins.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

<!-- CURSOR_SUMMARY -->
---

> [!NOTE]
> **Low Risk**
> Low risk refactor that centralizes how “disabled” components are detected; main risk is misclassifying a component as disabled and skipping constraint evaluation or builtin-use checks.
> 
> **Overview**
> Adds `CircuitEval::is_disabled()` (defaulting to `interaction_columns() == 0`) and replaces ad-hoc “no columns/enabled components” checks across the verifier.
> 
> Composition evaluation now skips disabled components explicitly (while marking sizes as maybe-unused), `verify` derives enable bits from `statement` components rather than `ProofConfig`, and Cairo builtin verification no longer depends on `ProofConfig`, using `component.is_disabled()` instead. `ProofConfig::from_components` also asserts disabled components have zero trace/interaction columns.

## Summary

Broad cleanup and refactoring across `data-model` and `runner`: schema hash dispatch improvements, value-hash renames, stableStringify removal, and ContentId→HashObject rename.

### Changes

1. **`hashSchemaPathSelector` → `hashSchemaItem(FabricValue)`** — generalize across all three dispatch layers (dispatch, legacy, modern) to cover all eventual non-schema hash uses (path selectors, value descriptors, etc.)

3. **`MapSetStringToStrings` and `MapSetStringToPathSelectors`** convenience subclasses

5. **Refactor `value-hash.ts` dispatch** from module-binding swap to inline flag checks (matching the pattern used elsewhere in data-model)
6. **Full `ContentId` → `HashObject` rename** — type, functions (`isContentId`→`isHashObject`), `NATIVE_TAGS.ContentId`→`NATIVE_TAGS.ContentHash`, internal constants
7. **`FabricHash.fromString()`** static method + `hashObjectFromString()` dispatch
8. **`contentIdFromJSON` → `hashObjectFromJson`** (casing fix)
9. **Export `LegacyHashObject` type**, hide `Reference` behind `isLegacyHashObject()` wrapper

title: refactor: rework SchemaAndHash, schema-hash returns FabricHash, schema interning
body:
## Summary

Rework `SchemaAndHash` and schema-hash dispatch to use `FabricHash` throughout, enforce deep-frozen schemas, add schema interning with bidirectional caching, and clean up related code.

### Changes

| Commit | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| `333d8c82c` | **Rework SchemaAndHash constructor:** Takes `(schema, hash: FabricHash)` instead of string hash. Adds `.hashString` getter. |
| `965edf15b` | **Constructor throws on non-deep-frozen schema:** Enforces immutability invariant at construction time. |
| `099f501ca` | **Schema-hash functions return `FabricHash`:** Legacy path uses `makeLegacyFabricHash("legacy")`. All runner call sites updated. |
| `e7944dbb5` | **Schema interning:** `internSchema()` and `findInternedSchema()` with bidirectional `WeakMap`/`WeakRef` cache. Boolean schemas handled separately. |
| `84a6e41be` | **FinalizationRegistry cleanup:** Removes dead `hashToRef` entries when interned schemas are GC'd. |
| `1b73a8fa2` | **Improved test coverage:** Non-empty `Uint8Array` values with distinct arrays; structurally-equal-but-not-identical schema test. |
| `0dbf28e96` | **`#private` fields with getters:** `SchemaAndHash` uses private fields instead of `readonly`. |
| `f6348c733` | **Cache rework:** Intern cache stores `SchemaAndHash` instances directly; boolean interning simplified; `findInternedSchema` returns cached `SchemaAndHash`. |
| `2763ee170` | **Remove redundant `.toString()`:** String concatenation calls it automatically. |
| `54c5b8543` | **Remove dead export:** `hashOfAsRawStringModern` no longer needed. |
| `392000ba7` | **Document cache behavior:** Unfrozen-schema cache behavior documented; short-circuit frozen inputs. |
| `5e82e9c03` | **Assert strict identity in tests:** `assertStrictEquals` for interning tests; seed boolean interns into `hashToSah`. |
| `37ee88a5b` | **Wipe intern cache on flag change:** Cache cleared when schema hash flag changes at runtime. |
| `6a167edbb` | **Remove redundant `isDeepFrozen` check:** `toDeepFrozenSchema()` already handles this internally. |
| `34470cfa9` | **Fix GC retention:** Split cache into `Map<string, WeakRef>` + `WeakMap<schema, SchemaAndHash>`. `SchemaAndHash` only reachable through WeakMap, so GC'd with schema. Boolean interns use sentinel objects as keys/targets. |
| `cd23b4fe3` | **Sentinel debugging visibility + test flag loop:** Sentinels use `{ cacheSentinel: true/false }` for debuggability. Tests wrapped in `describe()` loop over both flag values with `beforeAll`/`afterAll`. |
| `f1b5b7b43` | **Redirect `hashSchema()` through intern cache:** All schema hashing goes through the intern cache for deduplication. |
| `b0936f5f0` | **Remove unused imports:** Clean up unused `JSONSchema` imports from legacy/modern hash files. |
| `7a8ed9308` | **Do not cache mutable schemas by identity:** Intern cache only caches frozen schemas, preventing stale entries from mutated objects. |
| `f2d9803b5` | **Cache frozen schemas on hash-hit path:** `inputWasFrozen` guard ensures frozen inputs are cached even on hash-hit, avoiding repeated rehashing. |

title: feat: add `moose mcp` stdio proxy command for always-on AI agent integration
body:
The MCP server currently only runs inside `moose dev`, causing connection
failures when AI agents (e.g. Claude Code) start before the dev server.
This adds a new `moose mcp` command that runs a lightweight stdio-based
MCP proxy server that always starts successfully, advertises the same
tools, and proxies calls to the dev server when available.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

<!-- CURSOR_SUMMARY -->
---

> [!NOTE]
> **Medium Risk**
> Introduces a new long-running stdio server path and HTTP client proxying with timeouts/error handling; risk is mainly around connectivity/stdio framing and new dependency feature flags rather than core data/auth logic.
> 
> **Overview**
> Adds a new `moose mcp` CLI subcommand that runs an always-starting *stdio* MCP server, auto-resolving the dev server `host`/`port` (CLI args → project config → defaults) and proxying `call_tool` requests to the dev server’s `/mcp` endpoint with connection/tool-call timeouts and user-friendly failure messages.
> 
> Refactors MCP tool advertisement to a shared `all_tool_definitions()` list so the dev MCP server and the new proxy stay in sync, and updates docs to recommend the stdio proxy for Claude Code while keeping direct HTTP setup as an alternative.
> 
> Bumps Rust deps (`reqwest` 0.13.1→0.13.2 and wasm-bindgen/web-sys/js-sys family) and expands `rmcp` features to include client + stdio/reqwest transports; JS lockfile updates add platform `libc` metadata.
>

title: refactor[venom]: remove `fix_memlocs` pass
body:
_co-authored by claude opus 4.6_

### What I did

Remove the `FixMemLocationsPass` and replace it with a compile-time assertion that no concrete literal memory addresses exist in the IR before optimization.

Tighten the `VenomBuilder` API so memory-destination operands require `IRVariable` (alloca-derived pointers) instead of the looser `Operand` type.

### How I did it

- Delete `vyper/venom/passes/fix_mem_locations.py` and remove it from all optimization pipelines (O2, O3, Os).
- Add `check_mem_ops()` in `check_venom.py` that asserts no `IRLiteral` memory read/write targets exist before passes run.
- Change `VenomBuilder` signatures for `mload`, `mstore`, `mcopy`, `dloadbytes`, `istore`, `call`/`staticcall`/`delegatecall` pointer args, and `create`/`create2` offset from `Operand` → `IRVariable`.
- Update all codegen callsites in `codegen_venom/` (context, expr, stmt, module, builtins) to produce `IRVariable` pointers where literals were previously used.
- Remove the now-unused `Allocation.pinned` field.

### How to verify it

Full test suite: `pytest --experimental-codegen -m "not fuzzing"`

### Commit message

```
the `FixMemLocationsPass` was a post-hoc fixup that replaced hardcoded
memory offsets (FREE_VAR_SPACE, FREE_VAR_SPACE2) with alloca-relative
pointers. this was necessary because the legacy IR-to-venom translation
emitted raw literal addresses for scratch memory, which the allocator
could not track or relocate.

with the new direct AST-to-venom codegen (`codegen_venom/`), all memory
operations go through VenomBuilder, which already emits alloca-based
pointers. the fixup pass has no remaining work to do.

remove the pass entirely and replace it with an assertion in
`check_venom.py` (`check_mem_ops`) that verifies no concrete literal
memory reads or writes exist before the optimization pipeline runs. this
turns a silent correctness risk into a hard failure at compile time.

additionally, tighten the VenomBuilder API so that memory-destination
operands (`mload`, `mstore`, `mcopy`, `dloadbytes`, `istore`,
`call`/`staticcall`/`delegatecall` pointer args, `create`/`create2`
offset) require `IRVariable` instead of the looser `Operand` type. this
makes it a type error to pass a literal address where an alloca-derived
pointer is expected, catching the class of bugs that
`FixMemLocationsPass` was designed to paper over.
```

### Description for the changelog

Remove `FixMemLocationsPass` — all memory operations now use alloca-derived pointers by construction. Added compile-time assertion and tightened builder type signatures to prevent regressions.

title: Serialize all claimed sums regardless of enable bits
body:
Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

<!-- CURSOR_SUMMARY -->
---

> [!NOTE]
> **Medium Risk**
> Changes the proof byte format by always serializing/deserializing `Claim::claimed_sums` for all components (not just enabled ones), which can break compatibility with previously serialized proofs and any consumers expecting the old size/layout.
> 
> **Overview**
> Updates proof serialization to **always include all component claimed sums** regardless of enable bits, and adjusts deserialization and `ProofInfo::from_config` sizing to match the new fixed-length claim layout.
> 
> Refactors the API by removing `serialize_proof_with_config` in favor of `CircuitSerialize` impls on `Proof<QM31>` and `Claim<QM31>`, and drops now-unused deps (`itertools`, `num-traits`) with corresponding test updates.
